items.question: Broadcast stations must offer federal candidates the lowest unit rate for ads when what condition is met?
items.correctAnswer: the ads run within a defined window before a primary or general election
incorrectAnswers: the candidate buys only cable channels
the ads run only on weekends
the candidate has no party affiliation
items.explanation: FCC rules give qualified federal candidates discounted lowest rates during the statutory pre-election period.

items.question: Federal law bars contributions to federal candidates or committees from whom?
items.correctAnswer: foreign nationals who are not lawful permanent residents
items.explanation: Foreign nationals without green cards may not donate to federal campaigns; citizens and lawful permanent residents may be allowed to donate within limits.

items.question: Independent expenditures are spending that must be what, compared with a candidate's own campaign?
items.correctAnswer: not coordinated with the candidate or campaign
incorrectAnswers: approved by the Senate Ethics Committee
items.explanation: Independent spending is supposed to be separate from a candidate's committee; coordination can change how rules apply.

items.question: The Presidential Election Campaign Fund offers optional public grants for qualified general-election nominees if they agree to what?
items.correctAnswer: limit spending to the grant amount and follow fund rules
incorrectAnswers: accept no private donations of any kind ever
items.explanation: Public funding for general-election nominees is voluntary and comes with spending limits for those who accept it.

items.question: What must most federal candidate committees and party committees do with contributions they receive?
items.correctAnswer: report them to the FEC on a public schedule
incorrectAnswers: deposit them only in personal bank accounts
report them only to state courts
keep amounts secret until after the election
items.explanation: Disclosure laws require reporting so voters can see who funds federal campaigns.

items.question: Which federal agency enforces limits and disclosure rules for money in federal elections?
items.explanation: Congress created the FEC to administer and enforce the Federal Election Campaign Act for federal races.
